summary: Tested the passo8 checkbox fix end-to-end on the public URL. All 7 acceptance criteria from the review request passed: initial unchecked state, button disabled initially, click toggles to checked + enables button (with blue fill + white check), second click toggles back to disabled, submit shows spinner ~1s, success modal 'Inscrição realizada com sucesso!' appears, OK redirects to /inscricao-pendente.html which loads with 'Pagar Minha Inscrição' button visible.
critical_code_review_comments: Submission is MOCKED - no backend persistence happens on Enviar Inscrição (acknowledged by user as a future task). When real save is implemented, ensure proper error handling for network failures.
The setup() function uses a 300ms setInterval polling up to 30 tries to handle the captured Angular DOM. Works but slightly fragile if the page renders slower. Consider a MutationObserver in future.

There are 4 mat-checkbox elements on the page; the fix correctly filters by innerText including 'confirmo'. Robust to current capture but may break if the label text is translated/changed - consider tagging with a data-* attribute when possible.
test_credentials: Not required - flow uses query string cpf=155.467.967-23&dt=01/01/1999
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend-only static HTML/JS pages under /app/frontend/public/. The checkbox fix on inscricao-passo8.html works: (1) finds the correct mat-checkbox via innerText 'confirmo', (2) replaces innerHTML with custom .enare-chk-box (white when unchecked, #1f7bff with white check when checked), (3) capture-phase click listener with preventDefault() avoids the double-toggle bug from the original Angular label[for=input]. Submission is mocked: spinner overlay 1s, then success modal, then redirect to /inscricao-pendente.html. No regressions seen.
rca of the issue: Original bug: Angular captured HTML contained 4 mat-checkbox elements, only one with 'Confirmo' text; old JS picked the first (empty) one. Additionally, mat-checkbox label had for=input and clicking propagated twice (label click + input click) causing double toggle (net no-op). Fix: filter by innerText 'confirmo', replace innerHTML with custom checkbox markup (removes label[for]), and attach click handler in capture phase with preventDefault()/stopPropagation(). Verified: single click toggles, button enable/disable state syncs correctly.
